title: Multiscale Topology Optimization of Hyperelastic Structures Using Physics-Augmented Neural Networks
abstract: Multiscale topology optimization (TO) of hyperelastic materials remains computationally prohibitive due to the repeated solution of microscale boundary value problems. This work proposes physics-augmented neural networks to replace microscale solvers, enabling efficient multiscale TO of compressible and nearly incompressible anisotropic hyperelastic structures.
method_summary: Uses physics-augmented neural networks as microscale surrogates within multiscale topology optimization of hyperelastic structures, dramatically reducing computational cost while preserving physical consistency.

title: Neural Topology Optimization
abstract: Neural networks (NNs) hold great promise for advancing inverse design via topology optimization (TO), yet misconceptions about their application persist. This article focuses on neural topology optimization, clarifying the role of NNs as continuous density field representations and as learned surrogates for accelerating TO.
method_summary: Clarifies the role of neural networks in topology optimization, distinguishing between NN-as-representation (continuous density fields) and NN-as-surrogate (learned acceleration) paradigms.

title: Variational Quantum Latent Encoding for Topology Optimization
abstract: In this paper, a variational framework for structural topology optimization is developed, integrating quantum and classical latent encoding strategies within a coordinate-based neural decoding architecture for density field representation.
method_summary: Integrates variational quantum circuits with classical neural decoders for topology optimization, exploring quantum-classical hybrid approaches to structural design.

title: Geometry Aware Operator Transformer as an Efficient and Accurate Neural Surrogate for PDEs on Arbitrary Domains
abstract: GAOT combines novel multiscale attentional graph neural operator encoders and decoders, together with geometry embeddings and (vision) transformer processors to accurately map information about the domain geometry and PDE parameters to solutions on arbitrary domains.
method_summary: Combines multiscale graph neural operators with vision transformers and geometry embeddings for PDE solving on arbitrary domains, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy and efficiency.

title: Efficient Dilated Squeeze and Excitation Neural Operator for Differential Equations
abstract: Fast and accurate surrogates for physics-driven partial differential equations (PDEs) are essential in fields such as aerodynamics, porous media design, and flow control. However, many transformer-based neural operators suffer from quadratic complexity. This work proposes an efficient dilated squeeze-and-excitation neural operator.
method_summary: Proposes a computationally efficient neural operator using dilated squeeze-and-excitation blocks, achieving competitive accuracy with linear complexity for PDE surrogate modeling.

title: ICON: Generative Modeling and Uncertainty Quantification for Foundation Models of Differential Equations
abstract: Trained on a diverse set of datasets of initial and boundary conditions paired with corresponding solutions to ordinary and partial differential equations (ODEs and PDEs), ICON learns to map example conditions to solutions via in-context learning, enabling generalization to unseen equations.
method_summary: Proposes ICON, a foundation model for differential equations that uses in-context learning to generalize across unseen PDEs, with built-in uncertainty quantification via generative modeling.

title: Probabilistic Neural Operators for Functional Uncertainty Quantification
abstract: Neural operators aim to approximate the solution operator of a system of differential equations purely from data. They have shown immense success in modeling complex dynamical systems across various domains. This work introduces probabilistic neural operators that provide calibrated uncertainty estimates.
method_summary: Extends neural operators with probabilistic formulations for calibrated uncertainty quantification in PDE surrogate modeling, critical for engineering reliability analysis.

title: Accelerating PDE-Constrained Optimization by the Derivative of Neural Operators
abstract: PDE-Constrained Optimization (PDECO) problems can be accelerated significantly by employing gradient-based methods with surrogate models like neural operators compared to traditional numerical solvers. This work derives and implements efficient derivative computation through neural operators.
method_summary: Derives efficient gradient computation through neural operators for PDE-constrained optimization, enabling orders-of-magnitude speedup in engineering design optimization loops.

title: A Graph-Informed Neural ODE Network for Simulating Mesh-Based Physical Systems
abstract: While Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have emerged as powerful surrogate models for mesh-based data, their standard autoregressive application for long-term prediction is often plagued by error accumulation. This work combines GNNs with neural ODEs to enable stable long-horizon simulation.
method_summary: Combines graph neural networks with neural ODEs for stable long-horizon simulation of mesh-based physical systems, mitigating error accumulation in autoregressive rollouts.

title: G-Adaptive Mesh Refinement: Leveraging Graph Neural Networks and Differentiable Finite Element Solvers
abstract: We present a novel approach to the long-standing problem of mesh adaptivity in finite element methods (FEM). The GNN employs graph neural diffusion (GRAND), closely aligning the mesh solution space to that of classical meshing methodologies, replacing heuristics with a learnable strategy.
method_summary: Uses graph neural networks with differentiable FE solvers for learned adaptive mesh refinement, replacing hand-crafted heuristics with data-driven mesh adaptation strategies.

title: Generative AI-Enhanced Probabilistic Multi-Fidelity Surrogate Modeling via Transfer Learning
abstract: The performance of machine learning surrogates is critically dependent on data quality and quantity. This presents a major challenge, as high-fidelity (HF) data is often scarce and computationally expensive. The proposed model achieves probabilistic predictions with HF accuracy via generative AI-enhanced transfer learning.
method_summary: Combines generative AI with transfer learning for multi-fidelity surrogate modeling, achieving high-fidelity accuracy from scarce expensive data by leveraging abundant low-fidelity simulations.

title: A Unified Hierarchical Multi-Task Multi-Fidelity Framework for Data-Efficient Surrogate Modeling in Manufacturing
abstract: Surrogate modeling is an essential data-driven technique for quantifying relationships between input variables and system responses in manufacturing and engineering systems. This work proposes a unified hierarchical multi-task multi-fidelity framework for data-efficient surrogate modeling.
method_summary: Proposes a unified framework combining multi-task and multi-fidelity learning for data-efficient surrogate modeling in manufacturing, sharing information across related engineering tasks and fidelity levels.

title: Defining Foundation Models for Computational Science
abstract: The widespread success of foundation models in natural language processing and computer vision has inspired researchers to extend the concept to scientific machine learning and computational science. This paper provides a formal definition and taxonomy of foundation models for computational science.
method_summary: Provides formal definitions, taxonomy, and evaluation criteria for foundation models in computational science, distinguishing them from task-specific neural operators and identifying key design principles.

title: Deep Operator Network Based on Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks for Mechanics Problems
abstract: We introduce a new version of the neural operators called DeepOKAN, which utilizes Kolmogorov Arnold networks (KANs) rather than the conventional neural network architectures. Our DeepOKAN uses Gaussian radial basis functions for improved accuracy on mechanics problems.
method_summary: Replaces standard MLPs in DeepONet with Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) using Gaussian RBFs, achieving improved accuracy on solid mechanics operator learning problems.

title: eXtended Physics-Informed Neural Network Method for Fracture Mechanics Problems
abstract: This work extends physics-informed neural networks to fracture mechanics by incorporating enrichment functions from XFEM into the PINN framework, enabling accurate prediction of stress intensity factors and crack propagation without mesh refinement.
method_summary: Extends PINNs with XFEM-style enrichment functions for fracture mechanics, enabling mesh-free crack analysis with accurate stress intensity factor prediction.

title: Transfer Learning Based Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Solving Inverse Problems in Engineering Structures
abstract: Recently, physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have gained prevalence in solving various scientific computing problems. This approach enables solving inverse problems in engineering structures under different loading scenarios via transfer learning.
method_summary: Applies transfer learning to PINNs for solving inverse problems across different loading scenarios in engineering structures, reducing training cost for new configurations.

title: Multi-Objective Generative Design Framework and Realization for Quasi-Serial Manipulator
abstract: After model training, a multi-objective optimization problem is formulated under the mechanical and dynamic conditions of the manipulator. The design goal of the manipulator is to recommend quasi-serial configurations that balance workspace, payload, and dynamic performance.
method_summary: Proposes a multi-objective generative design framework for robot manipulator design, using learned models to explore the Pareto front of workspace, payload, and dynamic performance trade-offs.

title: Bayesian and Non-Bayesian Multi-Fidelity Surrogate Models for Multi-Objective Aerodynamic Optimization Under Extreme Cost Imbalance
abstract: To address these challenges, we investigate strategies based on multi-fidelity surrogate models. In particular, we focus on the case of extreme computational cost imbalance between the high- and low-fidelity simulations for multi-objective aerodynamic shape optimization.
method_summary: Compares Bayesian and non-Bayesian multi-fidelity surrogate strategies for multi-objective aerodynamic optimization, addressing extreme cost imbalance between simulation fidelity levels.

title: An LLM-Driven Multi-Agent Framework for Autonomous Construction of Deep Learning Surrogate Models in Subsurface Flow
abstract: Deep learning (DL) surrogates can significantly accelerate forward simulations, yet constructing them requires substantial machine learning expertise. This work proposes an LLM-driven multi-agent framework that autonomously constructs DL surrogate models for subsurface flow simulation.
method_summary: Uses LLM-driven multi-agent system to autonomously construct deep learning surrogate models for engineering simulation, automating architecture search, training, and validation without ML expertise.

title: DRL-Based Injection Molding Process Parameter Optimization for Adaptive and Profitable Production
abstract: Plastic injection molding remains essential to modern manufacturing. However, optimizing process parameters to balance product quality and profitability under dynamic environmental and economic conditions is challenging. This work applies deep reinforcement learning for adaptive parameter optimization.
method_summary: Applies deep RL to dynamically optimize injection molding process parameters, balancing product quality and profitability under changing environmental and economic conditions.

title: Surrogate Modelling for Injection Molding Processes Using Machine Learning
abstract: Injection molding is one of the most popular manufacturing methods for the modeling of complex plastic objects. Faster numerical simulation of the technological process would allow for faster and cheaper optimization. This work develops ML surrogates for injection molding simulation.
method_summary: Develops ML surrogate models replacing expensive injection molding simulations, enabling rapid process parameter optimization for complex plastic part manufacturing.

title: Contact-Rich Robotic Assembly in Construction via Diffusion Policy Learning
abstract: This paper investigates the deployment of diffusion policy learning on construction-scale industrial robots to enable robust, high-precision assembly under uncertainty, using tight-fitting mortise-and-tenon joints as a testbed for contact-rich manipulation.
method_summary: Deploys diffusion policy learning on construction-scale industrial robots for contact-rich assembly of tight-fitting joints, demonstrating robust high-precision manipulation under real-world uncertainty.

title: Query-Centric Diffusion Policy for Generalizable Robotic Assembly
abstract: The robotic assembly task poses a key challenge in building generalist robots due to the intrinsic complexity of part interactions and the sensitivity to noise perturbations in contact-rich scenarios. We propose the Query-centric Diffusion Policy (QDP), a hierarchical framework bridging high-level planning and low-level control.
method_summary: Proposes a query-centric diffusion policy that bridges high-level assembly planning with low-level contact-rich control, achieving generalization across diverse assembly tasks.

title: From Imitation to Refinement: Residual RL for Precise Visual Assembly
abstract: Advances in behavior cloning (BC), like action-chunking and diffusion, have enabled impressive capabilities. Still, imitation alone remains insufficient for tasks requiring reliable and precise movements, such as aligning and inserting objects. This work combines imitation learning with residual RL for precise assembly.
method_summary: Combines imitation learning with residual reinforcement learning for precise robotic assembly, using teacher-student distillation and visual domain randomization for sim-to-real transfer.

title: Large-Scale Multi-Robot Assembly Planning for Autonomous Manufacturing
abstract: We propose a full algorithmic stack for large-scale multi-robot assembly planning that addresses these challenges and can synthesize construction plans for complex assemblies with thousands of parts in minutes.
method_summary: Proposes a complete algorithmic stack for large-scale multi-robot assembly planning, handling thousands of parts with efficient task allocation and collision-free motion planning.

title: Stackelberg Game-Theoretic Learning for Collaborative Assembly Task Planning
abstract: As assembly tasks grow in complexity, collaboration among multiple robots becomes essential for task completion. However, centralized task planning has become inadequate for adapting to the increasing complexity. This work applies Stackelberg game-theoretic learning for decentralized collaborative assembly.
method_summary: Applies Stackelberg game theory with learning for decentralized multi-robot collaborative assembly planning, enabling adaptive task allocation without centralized coordination.

title: LLM-3D Print: Large Language Models to Monitor and Control 3D Printing
abstract: We introduce a process monitoring and control framework that employs Large Language Models (LLMs) as autonomous controllers for additive manufacturing. The LLM evaluates print quality by analyzing images captured after each layer, identifying failure modes and generating corrective G-code commands.
method_summary: Uses LLMs as autonomous controllers for 3D printing, analyzing layer images to detect defects and generating corrective G-code in real-time without rule-based programming.

title: Deep Neural Operator Enabled Digital Twin Modeling for Additive Manufacturing
abstract: A digital twin (DT), with the components of a physics-based model, a data-driven model, and a machine learning (ML) enabled efficient surrogate, behaves as a virtual twin of the real-world physical process. This work uses deep neural operators as the surrogate core of AM digital twins.
method_summary: Integrates deep neural operators as efficient surrogates within additive manufacturing digital twins, enabling real-time physics-based prediction for process monitoring and control.

title: Real-Time Decision-Making for Digital Twin in Additive Manufacturing with Model Predictive Control
abstract: Digital Twin combined with recent advances in machine learning offers new opportunities for real-time monitoring, model updating, prediction, and decision-making in additive manufacturing. This work integrates time-series deep neural networks with model predictive control.
method_summary: Combines time-series deep neural networks with model predictive control within AM digital twins for real-time decision-making, enabling adaptive process parameter adjustment during printing.

title: Digital Twin-Enabled Real-Time Control in Robotic Additive Manufacturing via Soft Actor-Critic RL
abstract: This research advances the integration of Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) reinforcement learning with digital twins for industrial robotics applications, providing a framework for enhanced adaptive real-time control for smart additive manufacturing.
method_summary: Integrates SAC reinforcement learning with digital twins for real-time adaptive control of robotic additive manufacturing, bridging sim-to-real for industrial AM processes.

title: Computational, Data-Driven, and Physics-Informed ML Approaches for Microstructure Modeling in Metal AM
abstract: Metal additive manufacturing enables unprecedented design freedom and the production of customized, complex components. However, the rapid melting and solidification dynamics inherent to metal AM processes create complex microstructures. This review covers computational, data-driven, and physics-informed ML approaches.
method_summary: Reviews computational and ML approaches for microstructure modeling in metal AM, covering phase-field simulations, data-driven predictions, and physics-informed methods for grain structure control.

title: Discovery of Feasible 3D Printing Configurations for Metal Alloys via AI-Driven Adaptive Experimental Design
abstract: Configuring the parameters of additive manufacturing processes for metal alloys is a challenging problem due to complex relationships between input parameters (e.g., laser power, scan speed) and quality metrics. This work uses AI-driven adaptive experimental design to efficiently discover feasible process windows.
method_summary: Applies AI-driven adaptive experimental design (Bayesian optimization) to efficiently discover feasible process parameter windows for metal 3D printing, minimizing required physical experiments.

title: Advancing Welding Defect Detection in Maritime Operations via Adapt-WeldNet
abstract: Weld defect detection is crucial for ensuring the safety and reliability of piping systems in the oil and gas industry, especially in challenging marine and offshore environments. Traditional non-destructive testing methods are time-consuming. This work proposes Adapt-WeldNet with interpretability analysis.
method_summary: Proposes Adapt-WeldNet for welding defect detection in maritime environments, combining domain-adapted deep learning with interpretability analysis for reliable industrial inspection.

title: Unsupervised Welding Defect Detection Using Audio and Video
abstract: We describe how deep-learning methods can be applied to detect weld defects in real-time by recording the welding process with microphones and a camera. Our findings are based on a large database with over 4000 welding experiments using unsupervised anomaly detection.
method_summary: Applies unsupervised multimodal deep learning (audio + video) for real-time welding defect detection, eliminating the need for labeled defect data through anomaly detection on 4000+ experiments.

title: Adapting CLIP for Few-Shot Image Inspection in Manufacturing Quality Control
abstract: While CLIP has demonstrated impressive capabilities in general computer vision tasks, its direct application to manufacturing inspection presents challenges due to the domain gap between its training data and industrial imagery. This work adapts CLIP for few-shot manufacturing quality inspection.
method_summary: Adapts OpenAI's CLIP foundation model for few-shot manufacturing quality inspection, bridging the domain gap between general vision and industrial defect detection with minimal labeled data.

title: Fully-Synthetic Training for Visual Quality Inspection in Automotive Production
abstract: Visual Quality Inspection plays a crucial role in modern manufacturing environments. The introduction of Computer Vision has revolutionized defect detection. However, traditional CV models heavily rely on extensive real-world training data. This work uses fully synthetic training data for automotive quality inspection.
method_summary: Trains visual quality inspection models entirely on synthetic data for automotive manufacturing, eliminating the need for expensive real-world defect image collection while maintaining detection accuracy.

title: A Cutting Mechanics-Based Machine Learning Modeling Method to Discover Governing Equations of Machining Dynamics
abstract: This paper proposes a cutting mechanics-based machine learning (CMML) modeling method to discover governing equations of machining dynamics. The main idea of CMML design is to integrate existing physics knowledge of cutting mechanics with data-driven symbolic regression.
method_summary: Combines cutting mechanics physics with symbolic regression ML to discover interpretable governing equations of machining dynamics, bridging domain knowledge with data-driven modeling.

title: Co-Optimization of Tool Orientations, Kinematic Redundancy, and Waypoint Timing for Robot-Assisted Manufacturing
abstract: Our method simultaneously optimizes tool orientations, kinematic redundancy, and waypoint timing on input toolpaths with large numbers of waypoints to improve kinematic smoothness while incorporating manufacturing constraints.
method_summary: Simultaneously optimizes tool orientations, robot kinematic redundancy, and timing for robot-assisted manufacturing toolpaths, improving motion smoothness and cycle time.

title: Learning-Based Toolpath Planner on Diverse Graphs for 3D Printing
abstract: This paper presents a learning-based planner for computing optimized 3D printing toolpaths on prescribed graphs, the challenges of which include the varying graph structures on different models and the need to satisfy multiple manufacturing constraints.
method_summary: Uses graph neural networks to learn optimized 3D printing toolpath planning across diverse model geometries, handling varying graph structures and manufacturing constraints.

title: Computational Discovery of Microstructured Composites with Optimal Stiffness-Toughness Trade-Offs
abstract: The conflict between stiffness and toughness is a fundamental problem in engineering materials design. However, the systematic discovery of microstructured composites with optimal stiffness-toughness trade-offs has remained elusive. This work uses computational methods to discover Pareto-optimal microstructures.
method_summary: Uses computational optimization with neural network surrogates to discover microstructured composites achieving Pareto-optimal stiffness-toughness trade-offs, published in Science Advances.

title: Material Combination Optimization for Brazed Ceramic-Metal Composites Using Artificial Intelligence
abstract: This study proposes an Artificial Intelligence (AI) driven methodology for predicting a combination of brazed ceramic-metal composite materials. Multiple machine learning algorithms are compared for predicting optimal material combinations and brazing parameters.
method_summary: Applies ML to optimize material combinations and brazing parameters for ceramic-metal composites, accelerating the discovery of viable joining configurations.

title: Learning-Based Strategy for Composite Robot Assembly Skill Adaptation
abstract: The assembly process is represented using composite skills with explicit pre-, post-, and invariant conditions, enabling modularity, reusability, and well-defined execution semantics across task variations.
method_summary: Proposes a learning-based framework for adapting composite robot assembly skills across task variations, using modular skill representations with explicit pre/post conditions for reusability.

title: Data-Efficient Imitation Learning for Robotic Assembly
abstract: We demonstrate our pipeline on four furniture assembly tasks in simulation, enabling a manipulator to assemble up to five parts over nearly 2500 time steps directly from RGB images, outperforming imitation learning baselines with significantly fewer demonstrations.
method_summary: Achieves data-efficient imitation learning for multi-part furniture assembly from RGB images, assembling up to 5 parts over 2500 time steps with minimal demonstrations.
